See PROXY_OPTIONS for more.","errors":{"AddressEmptyCode(address)":[{"details":"There's no code at `target` (it is not a contract)."}],"ERC1967InvalidImplementation(address)":[{"details":"The `implementation` of the proxy is invalid."}],"ERC1967NonPayable()":[{"details":"An upgrade function sees `msg.value > 0` that may be lost."}],"FailedCall()":[{"details":"A call to an address target failed. The target may have reverted."}],"InvalidInitialization()":[{"details":"The contract is already initialized."}],"NotInitializing()":[{"details":"The contract is not initializing."}],"RequestKeyConflict(bool,uint40,uint40,bytes16,bytes16)":[{"details":"Occurs during a request where (`reqId` is reused and/or `seqNum` < expected) and the prior call had a different key (caller,seqNum,reqId) so replay does not occur as it otherwise could","params":{"isNewReqId":"true: `reqIdAct` is new; false: value was reused with a different (caller,seqNum)","reqIdAct":"Actual request id received","reqIdExp":"Expected request id, based upon cache lookup by (caller,seqNum); Zero-value if !`isNewReqId`","seqNumAct":"Actual sequence number received","seqNumExp":"Expected sequence number based upon caller"}}],"SeqNumGap(uint40,uint40,address)":[{"details":"Occurs when seq num `rcvd` > `expect`, possible causes: error from network/provider/client/reorg     - Invariant: `seqNum` must increment monotonically for a write call, see table above","params":{"caller":"Caller's address","seqNumAct":"Received sequence number","seqNumExp":"Expected sequence number"}}],"UUPSUnauthorizedCallContext()":[{"details":"The call is from an unauthorized context."}],"UUPSUnsupportedProxiableUUID(bytes32)":[{"details":"The storage `slot` is unsupported as a UUID."}]},"events":{"Initialized(uint64)":{"details":"Triggered when the contract has been initialized or reinitialized."},"ReqAck(bytes16,address,uint40,(bytes16,uint16,uint16,uint16,uint40,uint40),bool)":{"details":"A Request Acknowledgement Event occurs at the end of each request (if no revert) and bridges the gaps between data sources, clients, lossy/unreliable protocols/providers/neworks, and the on-chain call outcome - Captures 3 categories of data for off-chain calls:     - Key inputs (indexed params) allow a tx search via either:         - PK1: reqId: Allows a tx search on a single field such as to fill a gap on replay         - PK2: (caller,seqNum): Allows a tx search to audit a sequence for a caller     - Context: `replay` and `callRes.blockNum` provide context on when/where this happened     - Output: `callRes` provides fields (rc, lrc, count) for either a simple result or summary         - Additional results can be found in the tx receipt logs from prior events in the same tx","params":{"callRes":"See `CallRes`; `reqId` and `ReqAck` are separate params for indexes","caller":"Account initiating the tx","replay":"false for an original event, true when `reqId` was already processed","reqId":"From CallRes: Request ID, allows for gap-fill on replay","seqNum":"From CallRes: Caller's sequence number incremented monotonically"}},"Upgraded(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the implementation is upgraded."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"constructor":{"custom:api":"private","details":"Ensures the logic contract cannot be hijacked before the `initializer` runs - Sets version to `type(uint64).max` + `emit Initialized(version)` to prevent future initialization - `initialize` is where the business logic is initialized on proxies - For more info see comments in 'Initializable.sol'"},"getCallResByReqId(bytes16)":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Get a result for a prior write function call invoked from off-chain by this caller - Subject to storage lag when retrieved off-chain shortly after write, prefer the `ReqAck` event in that case","params":{"reqId":"Request ID of a prior call"},"returns":{"cr":"See `CallRes`"}},"getCallResBySeqNum(uint40)":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Get a result for a prior write function call invoked from off-chain by this caller - Subject to storage lag when retrieved off-chain shortly after write, prefer the `ReqAck` event in that case","params":{"seqNum":"Sequence number of a prior call by this caller"},"returns":{"cr":"See `CallRes`"}},"getContract(uint8)":{"details":"Get a contract address","params":{"contractId":"Contract ID requested"},"returns":{"_0":"Requested contract address"}},"getPropHdr(uint256)":{"params":{"pid":"Proposal ID, identifies an existing proposal"}},"getSeqNum(address)":{"details":"Get next expected seq num for the given account address","params":{"account":"Address to lookup"},"returns":{"seqNumEx":"Next expected seq num to be passed to a write function call from `account`"}},"getTokenBalances(address,uint8,address[])":{"params":{"accounts":"Accounts to check","tokAddr":"Token address","tokType":"Controls how to query the token"},"returns":{"balances":"Token balance per account, 0 if unsupported token"}},"getVersion()":{"details":"Get the current version"},"getXferExecIndex(uint256)":{"params":{"pid":"Proposal ID, identifies an existing transfer proposal"}},"getXferLites(uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"count":"Items to get, 0 = [iBegin:] (may exceed gas), See `getXfersLen` and PAGE_REQUESTS.","iBegin":"Index in the array to start processing","pid":"Proposal ID, identifies an existing transfer proposal"},"returns":{"results":"requested range of items"}},"getXfers(uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"count":"Items to get, 0 = [iBegin:] (may exceed gas), See `getXfersLen` and PAGE_REQUESTS.","iBegin":"Index in the array to start processing","pid":"Proposal ID, identifies an existing transfer proposal"},"returns":{"results":"requested range of items"}},"getXfersLen(uint256)":{"params":{"pid":"Proposal ID, identifies an existing transfer proposal"}},"inTokenAdminList(address)":{"details":"Complements `updateTokenAdminList`, see return value","params":{"tokAddr":"The token to add/remove from list"},"returns":{"_0":"Whether the token is in the admin list"}},"initialize(address,bytes16)":{"custom:api":"protected","details":"Basically replaces the constructor in a proxy oriented contract - `initializer` modifier ensures this function can only be called once during deploy - See UUPS_UPGRADE_SEQ for details on how to upgrade this contract","params":{"creator":"Creator's address for access control during setup","reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers"}},"preUpgrade(uint40,bytes16,bytes16)":{"details":"Stage values to use in the next call to `__authorizeUpgrade`","params":{"reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","reqIdStage":"Param to stage","seqNumEx":"=0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`"}},"propAddXfers(uint40,bytes16,(uint256,uint256,uint256,(bytes16,address,address,uint64,uint8,uint256,bytes16,bytes16)[]))":{"custom:api":"public"},"propCreate(uint256,bytes16,(string,uint8,address,uint256),bool)":{"custom:api":"private","details":"Add a transfer proposal, this creates a stub that must be populated by additional calls - Inputs validated upstream - No `_isReqReplay` since this call cannot be executed directly from off-chain","params":{"isRevDist":"Whether this transfer is a revenue distribution (relates to balance tracking)","pid":"Proposal ID, always increases","reqId":"Request ID","ti":"TokenInfo, see struct definition; ERC-1155 transfers use the token id from the `Xfer` items in prop"}},"propExecute(uint256)":{"custom:api":"private","details":"Should be called until no pages remain, each call runs until work is complete or gas limit. - This is a hot path for gas usage, more focus given to gas - The execution cursor is internal and therefore safe for excessive calls - Xfers with insufficient funds will fail but this should not happen as checked in proposal creation - See ACCOUNT_ACCESS_LIST, MALICIOUS_TRANSFERS, and TRANSFER_FAILURE - Success: emits XfersProcessed, conditionally PropExecuted, - Success progress: Check progress via returns and/or `getXferExecIndex` - Access by role: Agent - No `_isReqReplay` since this call cannot be executed directly from off-chain","params":{"pid":"Proposal ID, identifies an existing transfer proposal"},"returns":{"result":"Indicates progress where `rc` is set from `ExecXferRc`"}},"propFinalize(uint256)":{"custom:api":"private","details":"Reviews progress of `propAddXfers` and marks request as fully uploaded","params":{"pid":"Proposal ID"},"returns":{"rc":"Indicates progress: - Ok       : Proposal upload complete - NoProp   : No proposal found by pid - BadTotal : Total qty in header differs from the sum of transfers"}},"propPruneXfers(uint40,bytes16,uint256,uint256[])":{"custom:api":"public","params":{"reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","seqNumEx":"=0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`","skips":"Xfer indexes to be skipped during `propExecute`"}},"proxiableUUID()":{"details":"Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier."},"setContract(uint40,bytes16,uint8,address)":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Less size than separate setters","params":{"contractId":"Identifies the contract being set","newProxy":"The new proxy address","reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","seqNumEx":"=0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`"}},"updateTokenAdminList(uint40,bytes16,address,bool)":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Call behaves as add and remove (tokAddr=0)","params":{"add":"true: Add to list; false: Remove from list","reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","seqNumEx":"=0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`","tokAddr":"The token to add/remove from list"}},"upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes)":{"custom:oz-upgrades-unsafe-allow-reachable":"delegatecall","details":"Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event."}},"title":"XferMgr: Token transfer manager","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"getPropHdr(uint256)":{"notice":"Get xfer proposal info excluding the xfers, call `getXfers` for all transfers"},"getTokenBalances(address,uint8,address[])":{"notice":"Feature is orthogonal to the contract but convenient for now Get each account token balance directly from the token contract. - NOT AN ACCOUNT VAULT BALANCE - Provides batch behavior not available directly on tokens to coalesce many txs into 1 - ERC-1155 not supported to save size as those tokens supports it directly via `IERC1155.balanceOfBatch`"},"getXferExecIndex(uint256)":{"notice":"Get a proposal's next transfer index to be executed, a lightweight alternative to `getProp`"},"getXferLites(uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Get a range of lightweight transfers"},"getXfers(uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Get a range of detailed transfers"},"getXfersLen(uint256)":{"notice":"Get the count of uploaded transfers - also the insertion index for the next upload"},"propExecute(uint256)":{"notice":"Execute an approved transfer proposal"},"propPruneXfers(uint40,bytes16,uint256,uint256[])":{"notice":"Used to skip transfers in an approved proposal that have failed, this is a defensive escape hatch where skipped transfers can be resolved in future proposals to ensure progress for the current proposal. - Ensure skipped transfers cascade skips to dependent transfers if relevant (eg A => B, B => C) Most likely use cases would be either: - A token that runs destination contract code on a malicious wallet (USDC does not), See MALICIOUS_TRANSFERS - Insufficient funds/access to transfer Such cases should be avoidable/resolvable in other ways but this is a backup/fail-safe mechanism to ensure progress in a proposal rather than abort mid-batch due to a small problem subset. - Success: emits XfersPruned - Success progress: Check progress via returns and/or `getXfers` - Failure effects are conditional: Check returns. - Access by role: Agent (Can deny a transfer here but not redirect it)"},"setContract(uint40,bytes16,uint8,address)":{"notice":"Set a contract's address"},"updateTokenAdminList(uint40,bytes16,address,bool)":{"notice":"Update the list of tokens where transfers may constructor.details: Ensures the logic contract cannot be hijacked before the `initializer` runs - Sets version to `type(uint64).max` + `emit Initialized(version)` to prevent future initialization - `initialize` is where the business logic is initialized on proxies - For more info see comments in 'Initializable.sol'

getCallResByReqId(bytes16).details: Get a result for a prior write function call invoked from off-chain by this caller - Subject to storage lag when retrieved off-chain shortly after write, prefer the `ReqAck` event in that case

params.reqId: Request ID of a prior call

getCallResBySeqNum(uint40).details: Get a result for a prior write function call invoked from off-chain by this caller - Subject to storage lag when retrieved off-chain shortly after write, prefer the `ReqAck` event in that case

params.seqNum: Sequence number of a prior call by this caller

params.pid: Proposal ID, identifies an existing proposal

getSeqNum(address).details: Get next expected seq num for the given account address

returns.seqNumEx: Next expected seq num to be passed to a write function call from `account`

params.tokType: Controls how to query the token

returns.balances: Token balance per account, 0 if unsupported token

params.pid: Proposal ID, identifies an existing transfer proposal

params.count: Items to get, 0 = [iBegin:] (may exceed gas), See `getXfersLen` and PAGE_REQUESTS.
params.iBegin: Index in the array to start processing
params.pid: Proposal ID, identifies an existing transfer proposal

params.count: Items to get, 0 = [iBegin:] (may exceed gas), See `getXfersLen` and PAGE_REQUESTS.
params.iBegin: Index in the array to start processing
params.pid: Proposal ID, identifies an existing transfer proposal
Proposal ID, identifies an existing transfer proposal

params.tokAddr: The token to add/remove from list

returns._0: Whether the token is in the admin list

initialize(address,bytes16).details: Basically replaces the constructor in a proxy oriented contract - `initializer` modifier ensures this function can only be called once during deploy - See UUPS_UPGRADE_SEQ for details on how to upgrade this contract

params.creator: Creator's address for access control during setup
params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers

preUpgrade(uint40,bytes16,bytes16).details: Stage values to use in the next call to `__authorizeUpgrade`

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

propCreate(uint256,bytes16,(string,uint8.details: Add a transfer proposal, this creates a stub that must be populated by additional calls - Inputs validated upstream - No `_isReqReplay` since this call cannot be executed directly from off-chain

params.isRevDist: Whether this transfer is a revenue distribution (relates to balance tracking)
params.ti: TokenInfo, see struct definition; ERC-1155 transfers use the token id from the `Xfer` items in prop

propExecute(uint256).details: Should be called until no pages remain, each call runs until work is complete or gas limit. - This is a hot path for gas usage, more focus given to gas - The execution cursor is internal and therefore safe for excessive calls - Xfers with insufficient funds will fail but this should not happen as checked in proposal creation - See ACCOUNT_ACCESS_LIST, MALICIOUS_TRANSFERS, and TRANSFER_FAILURE - Success: emits XfersProcessed, conditionally PropExecuted, - Success progress: Check progress via returns and/or `getXferExecIndex` - Access by role: Agent - No `_isReqReplay` since this call cannot be executed directly from off-chain

params.pid: Proposal ID, identifies an existing transfer proposal

returns.result: Indicates progress where `rc` is set from `ExecXferRc`

propFinalize(uint256).details: Reviews progress of `propAddXfers` and marks request as fully uploaded

returns.rc: Indicates progress: - Ok       : Proposal upload complete - NoProp   : No proposal found by pid - BadTotal : Total qty in header differs from the sum of transfers

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`
params.skips: Xfer indexes to be skipped during `propExecute`

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

updateTokenAdminList(uint40,bytes16,addr.details: Call behaves as add and remove (tokAddr=0)

params.add: true: Add to list; false: Remove from list
params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`
params.tokAddr: The token to add/remove from list

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

getPropHdr(uint256).notice: Get xfer proposal info excluding the xfers, call `getXfers` for all transfers

getTokenBalances(address,uint8,address[].notice: Feature is orthogonal to the contract but convenient for now Get each account token balance directly from the token contract. - NOT AN ACCOUNT VAULT BALANCE - Provides batch behavior not available directly on tokens to coalesce many txs into 1 - ERC-1155 not supported to save size as those tokens supports it directly via `IERC1155.balanceOfBatch`

getXferExecIndex(uint256).notice: Get a proposal's next transfer index to be executed, a lightweight alternative to `getProp`

getXferLites(uint256,uint256,uint256).notice: Get a range of lightweight transfers

getXfers(uint256,uint256,uint256).notice: Get a range of detailed transfers

getXfersLen(uint256).notice: Get the count of uploaded transfers - also the insertion index for the next upload

propPruneXfers(uint40,bytes16,uint256,ui.notice: Used to skip transfers in an approved proposal that have failed, this is a defensive escape hatch where skipped transfers can be resolved in future proposals to ensure progress for the current proposal. - Ensure skipped transfers cascade skips to dependent transfers if relevant (eg A => B, B => C) Most likely use cases would be either: - A token that runs destination contract code on a malicious wallet (USDC does not), See MALICIOUS_TRANSFERS - Insufficient funds/access to transfer Such cases should be avoidable/resolvable in other ways but this is a backup/fail-safe mechanism to ensure progress in a proposal rather than abort mid-batch due to a small problem subset. - Success: emits XfersPruned - Success progress: Check progress via returns and/or `getXfers` - Failure effects are conditional: Check returns. - Access by role: Agent (Can deny a transfer here but not redirect it)

updateTokenAdminList(uint40,bytes16,addr.notice: Update the list of tokens where transfers may mint/burn to prevent accidental value destruction - Tokens on this list are allowed to have xfers to/from AddrZero, else such transfers may fail pre-send - Useful for an internal token where mints/burns are routine/reversible but external tokens would destroy value
